content:
As someone who "completed the game" the anny lacked gameplay content. The raids were a total organisation failure, but even if they worked fine they were still the same enemy I already fought multiple times, with the same mechanics which I already encountered multiple times. I would have loved something more unique and potentially more challenging. Smashing the same eyes 15 times each was honestly boring and underwhelming for an anniversary event. 

On the other hand, Gmurphy is amazing and brings a lot of fresh air to the blue gameplay and I love how she completely changes the realm mechanic. I was dubious about the other lemurian requirements but, she can work completely fine in other teams as well. It's just that she is at her best in lemurian teams.
From my perspective it's like complaining that you should always bring Thais with ghelot. You don't need to bring those specific units together but if you do you are going to get better results. 

The rerun banners were amazing and I loved how you guys handled them. 

Despite the raids being a dumpster fire, their rewards were kinda amazing. 
The log in bonuses were also very good. 

Last but not least, the new arch was really fun. I still see people complaining about the final boss being too difficult, but I'm honestly all for it. As of now the only really difficult things in the game are 400pts+ Dtides and highest levels of phantasmal dives. The first one is fun and fine, I like Dtides, the other one I just don't do it because there are no rewards for doing it. 
Having the final boss of chapter 4 being actually challenging is cool for me. It's something for me to think about on how to defeat it gnosisless. 

If I had to give the anny a rating it would be something like this: 

Events gameplay: 0/10
New Story-gameplay: 8/10 
Rewards: 10/10 
New character and reruns: 10/10

It overalls to 7, which is how I feel. The good things were really good, the bad things were really bad. 
Anyways,
thanks for your hard work ❤️
am I crazy? I thought spamming the raids every day was pretty fun and gave me a motivation to log in and actually play rather than just login and sweep a couple stages and logout
People who don't have maxed pool characters can't 17 raids daily and miss out on rewards
You also have to pvp against other players in order to get the entries for the rewards

Also timezone and schedules IRL limit what you can do in the game, imagine raid start at like 3 am where you live, you wake up, see the boss almost dead but you can't do anything because you need to go to work or stuff like that and most likely the boss would be dead by the time it's launch break
It's not fun, it's frustrating, and if you calculate correctly it's like 128 pulls from raid events in total that you miss out because of stuff like that
Mmmm i love waking up at 2am on a school day to do raids

You also can't properly use mode auto because each team is now single use, your only few team might be able to beat it in auto but not the underleveled one even with the whale assist units that you'll eventually run out of since they are also mandatory + single use

idk never got that issue myself, yea the reset time was late, but could clear most raids think I did like 14-15 a day on average, just borrowing whale unit and auto all of them, think maybe 2 or 3 runs had to manual each day, wasn't hard tbh, I mean I think it could definitely be improved to not be a PvP event, but overall id much prefer actually have something to do than just do dtides every fortnight and login logout for 5min each day
Day 1, raid lasted for less than 7 hours if i remembers correctly day 2 was like 9 hours

You see the problem? You need to rush it because everyone is rushing too, it feels shit, it feels shit trying to do that also knowing other can't claim the rewards properly, it feels shit to miss out on those rewards too
Not increasing the amount of entries per raid so people won't FOMO as much but instead release 3 raids isn't helping at all too
Different stages, different rewards, some stages were cancerous to play with auto but had great rewards compared
I mean, if what you did is just pick a unit and auto, is that really an engaging event?
more engaging than login 5 min sweep a few stage claim reward logout ye
That doesn't mean that is good
i didnt say its good.,., i just said i had fun and it was engaging
thought i would say something since literally everyone else whos said about the raid all saying bad about it

I mean, if you find it fun, good for you.
But for a lot of people it really wasn't a pleasant experience, or at least nothing more than a chore, and that is not because of the gameplay itself, for the most part, but mostly because you had  to do it 17 times a day to get the theoretical maximum reward.
But still, all the points were already discussed at the time

It was a bad design tbh, also it looks like they also run a raid event in anniv 1.0, they should have seen all the criticism, 1.5 anniv also had raid but didn't had the problem of single use team, but people still complained so by now you would think they learned something, but in anniv 2.0 they triple it down with single use units
Yeah, that is another sour point for it, but this time we really told them what the problems were directly and clearly, so if they don't change stuff this time, then there is no excuse
It's a preference. I am fine with it, but can see the issues
i would have liked the raids if not for the pvp
i liked playing weird niche teams because all my good units were used up, felt like a dtide event kinda
but the pvp....made it more of a chore to get it done asap instead of leisurely playing through how i wanted?
